title: API Resources, Object Identity, Schemas, Desired State, and Status · Guided Manifest Studio
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working versioned API object contract for api resources, object identity, schemas, desired state, and status. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned API object contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve API group, version, kind, plural resource, scope, endpoint, and served or stored version for a concrete object.
Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence.
Use namespace, name, UID, resourceVersion, generation, timestamps, and managed fields to distinguish identity, concurrency, and history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-api-stability-deprecation-wixry4". Tailor its api-object.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Api Stability Deprecation into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation, keep K8s Api Stability Deprecation connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-api-stability-deprecation-wixry4
  - competency: k8s-api-stability-deprecation
    artifact: api-object.yaml
    decision: api stability deprecation
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: rotate a projected token while the API dependency slows
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: wixry4
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 3
      status-evidence: observedGeneration-and-conditions
    verify: kubectl explain deployment.spec --api-version=apps/v1 && kubectl get deployment status-api -o json
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only evidence, stale status, unknown fields, or an API selected without feature-state and deprecation review.
    repair: Use live discovery and schema, separate spec from status, compare generation with observedGeneration, and preserve object UID and resourceVersion.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A stable Kubernetes release makes every documented alpha and extension API safe for production.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate alpha, beta, stable, feature-gate, deprecation, removal, conversion, and version-skew evidence before selecting an API.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s Api Stability Deprecation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for K8s Api Stability Deprecation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop manifest is valid YAML but uses a removed API, contains an unknown field, and reports observedGeneration 4 behind generation 5. Establish discovery, schema, identity, spec, status, conditions, and compatibility evidence. Competency probe: Evaluate alpha, beta, stable, feature-gate, deprecation, removal, conversion, and version-skew evidence before selecting an API.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation, keep K8s Api Stability Deprecation connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate alpha, beta, stable, feature-gate, deprecation, removal, conversion, and version-skew evidence before selecting an API.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Api Stability Deprecation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop manifest is valid YAML but uses a removed API, contains an unknown field, and reports observedGeneration 4 behind generation 5. Establish discovery, schema, identity, spec, status, conditions, and compatibility evidence. Competency probe: Evaluate alpha, beta, stable, feature-gate, deprecation, removal, conversion, and version-skew evidence before selecting an API.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Api Stability Deprecation, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A stable Kubernetes release makes every documented alpha and extension API safe for production.
